matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: An observed CDKN2A variant places this sample in the curated CDKN2A research context. The bundled CDKN2A knowledge base is tumor-suppressor focused. Clinically important CDKN2A events are often rare germline variants, deletions, or promoter methylation changes rather than common low-effect polymorphisms. For CDKN2A, rare germline and somatic loss-of-function events usually carry more weight than common SNPs.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: CDKN2A is a major tumor-suppressor locus in melanoma, pancreatic cancer predisposition, glioma, and many additional tumors.
The locus is also central to aging and senescence research because p16INK4A expression is a canonical cellular-senescence marker.
Cancer-associated promoter methylation studies.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a CDKN2A variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. CDKN2A methylation is often biologically meaningful because promoter hypermethylation is a recurrent mode of tumor-suppressor silencing. In this workbench it should still be read as regulatory context rather than as a stand-alone diagnosis. The bundled CDKN2A knowledge base is tumor-suppressor focused. Clinically important CDKN2A events are often rare germline variants, deletions, or promoter methylation changes rather than common low-effect polymorphisms. For CDKN2A, rare germline and somatic loss-of-function events usually carry more weight than common SNPs.
